title: Why Angels Adam 3I
wikitext:
[]: Why Angels + Adam = 3I?             Quote Post by laurencium » Sun Aug 05, 2012 3:38 pm Why is it always presumed that an Angel coming into contact with Adam would cause Third Impact and the destruction of all humans? (For example, the Third Impact entry of the Wiki, and just generally throughout comments I see while lurking on this board). Is there any evidence I'm missing that would suggest this is the case? The only evidence we have for a giant life-ending catastrophe was the Second Impact (1I being just a crash landing on empty planet), and there it was something from Lilith + something from Adam. 2I makes sense here because the cause was at least a pseudo forbidden union. But why expect 3I from Angel+Adam? If that were the case, wouldn't it mean, by analogy, that humans (Lilim) coming into contact with Lilith would also cause 3I, which is clearly not the case? Perhaps because these kind of unions can only destroy the other kind of beings? Since Earth is already "Lilim-land", Lilim+Lilith wouldn't do anything, while Angel+Adam will kill all Lilims? But in that case couldn't they, when the first Angel appeared, just give a random human to Lilith and wipe out all the Angels instantly instead of having to fight them? I can understand if it's Kaworu + Adam = 3I, since Kaworu has Adam's soul and him contacting Adam would mean the complete Adam in full force, ridding Earth of Lilims. But any random Angel + Adam = 3I? I don't understand how it follows, especially since Lilim + Lilith doesn't do shit. I guess one could say Adam and Lilith are just different, so that's just the way it is, Angel+Adam=3I and Lilim+Lilith=nothing happens. But that doesn't seem to be very satisfying, certainly not more than just saying we actually don't know what's gonna happen when Angels contact Adam. Or is there something obvious that I'm missing?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Aug 06, 2012 2:22 am I have come to agree with this sentiment. I'm convinced that only Kaworu would have been able to cause an Impact with Adam, because he is already part Lilim. Some theories hold that his human body was a result of the DNA that "dived" into and physically fused with Adam, so him coming into contact with an intact Adam would be just like 2I all over again -- with the same DNA and everything. I think that it is likewise because of his body that he would be able to do nothing with Lilith, whereas if it had been any other Angel it would have been a different story (like in the NGE2 "Good Night" ending). (EDIT: More specifically, I think he lacks an S2 organ, which seems to be a key factor in all known versions of Third Impact.)

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Aug 06, 2012 4:52 pm Second Impact was Adam attempting to reset the planet (destroy all life) so it could fill the Earth with it's own Angelic children. The Angels are attempting to return to "mother" and revive her by fusing with it, allowing Adam to finish what it started. This is very bad for Lilimkind. But why expect 3I from Angel+Adam? If that were the case, wouldn't it mean, by analogy, that humans (Lilim) coming into contact with Lilith would also cause 3I, which is clearly not the case?

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Mon Aug 06, 2012 6:59 pm ^ Very informative post. Thank you! Some specifics in there I didn't know about...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Aug 07, 2012 11:51 am I recently proposed that the "Angel" part isn't necessarily what's important. Here's the particular bit: Reichu wrote: It might be the case that it's necessary to put a human who is not a Seed (and, by extension, not an Eva [= Seed precursor], either) in contact with a Seed in order to trigger a life-reducing event ("Impact") . The Seed, essentially, cannot minimize all human life to a purely spiritual state purely on her own whim: she must interface with a representative "normal human" (Lilin, Child of Adam, etc.) who provides the "catalytic willpower" to make this possible. For this reason, Yui and Lilith needed Shinji. Gendo would have provided a similar role, had things gone his way. Similarly, it could have been the psychological influence of the person used for the contact experiment with Adam that enabled Second Impact (as counterintuitive as that sounds). And an Angel who successfully triggered an Impact would have done so by providing this component.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Fri Aug 31, 2012 11:36 am I may be missing something huge, but... If Seele was trying to destroy the angels (presumably to ensure an Angel-induced Third Impact wouldn't happen), why would they send Tabris down to make contact with Lilith, if in fact that contact would cause 3I? EDIT - After thinking about it more, is it possible that the Angels weren't seeking to fuse with Lilith, but rather destroy her, making Adam the only progenitor on the planet?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Aug 31, 2012 11:42 am Merkaba wrote: I may be missing something huge, but... If Seele was trying to destroy the angels (presumably to ensure an angel-induced Third Impact wouldn't happen), why would they send Tabris down to make contact with Lilith, if in fact that contact would cause 3I?

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Fri Aug 31, 2012 12:00 pm So what was the worst that could happen if an Angel made contact with Lilith? I don't see Seele running the gambit of Tabris making contact, even if it is to fuck Shinji up, if s/he could actually induce an undesirable 3I.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Aug 31, 2012 1:09 pm Bagheera wrote: It wouldn't. That was kinda the point -- it was all one big fakeout to fuck Shinji up (whether or not Kaworu was playing along remains an open, and contentious, question).

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Aug 31, 2012 3:01 pm Hyper Shinchan: In the "Oyasumi" ending, it's very strongly implied that everyone has been turned into "Adamic" life. (The "sleep" part might be in reference to the incubation period.) As far as I understand the use of particles in the original 21' line , the donor participated in the experiment himself, i.e., personally made physical contact with Adam. Were this not the case, my guess is that they would have said that the experiment were with the genes , instead of being with the person (a "donor"). The idea about the 'non-Seed/-Eva human element as Impact catalyst' is simply based on the precedent we have. Even when Lilith and Adam are fused together, the Impact doesn't get moving until Shinji's soul is fully prepared as a "substrate".

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Aug 31, 2012 3:59 pm Reichu wrote: In the "Oyasumi" ending, it's very strongly implied that everyone has been turned into "Adamic" life. (The "sleep" part might be in reference to the incubation period.)

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Aug 31, 2012 4:04 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Even the fact that it was a fakeout remains an open question, Kaworu kept implying that he would have destroyed mankind if Shinji didn't kill him, also there's Rei stopping the Angel and putting all life, both Angels and Lilim, to rest in the case an Angel reached the Terminal Dogma in Eva2 (the Oyasumi ending).

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Aug 31, 2012 4:29 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: I guess that that line still remains quite vague though, also didn't they simply mention "genes" later?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Aug 31, 2012 4:40 pm Reichu wrote: He... doesn't know whether or not he intended to destroy the Lilin if Eva-02 hadn't been defeated?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Aug 31, 2012 5:06 pm Bagheera: I'm personally fine with taking Kaworu at his word that his actions were contingent on a 'roll of the dice' (i.e., whether Eva-02 was defeated or not). As far as the Angel ending goes -- again, I'm hesitant to draw conclusions. The event is not scripted such that it's explicit Rei was necessary in order for things to proceed. It's not as if we see the Angel try to fuse with Lilith / trigger the Impact and fail first. Rather, we see things proceeding without issue, and then Rei just shows up as if on cue, says mysterious things, and goes into Lilith with the Angel. Necessary data is missing.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Sep 01, 2012 2:31 am Is it possible that contacting Lilith is what CAUSED Rei to appear, in that ending? Rei's actions and thoughts imply that she has a subconscious awareness of her real self and atleast a psychological pull towards it. Mayhaps when her true self is stimulated by Impact shenanigans, she can't resist the pull any longer?

[]:             Quote Post by Linh » Wed Nov 21, 2012 10:46 am It's been in my mind until now that the cause of an Impact is "Seed soul/original body type A + Seed soul/original body type B" ( one of them is the original one ). For the 1I, it was Adam (Seed of Life) + Lilith (Seed of Wisdom), 2I was Adam (SoL) + random-human (Lilim=SoW), and then 3I was Rei ( WoL which she possessed from Gendo ) + Lilith (SoW). As for the Angel + Adam = Impact, I assume that it's not possible, since in 24, Kaworu was like: "Adam, our mother. Must all who were born of Adam return to Adam? Even at the cost of destroying humanity? No! This is... Lilith? I see, so that's what this is about, Lilin ." He started with questions and then came up with a realization of something after knowing the body was Lilith instead of Adam. Maybe that even Kaworu himself and the Angels did not know whether their union with Adam would create the Impact or not? And that theory was just a fabrication (probably by seele)? After all, these are just my personal predications, I could have missed something, since I've just watched TV serie and EoE only one time = =

[]:             Quote Post by Eliaskar » Wed Nov 21, 2012 12:29 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Is it possible that contacting Lilith is what CAUSED Rei to appear, in that ending? Rei's actions and thoughts imply that she has a subconscious awareness of her real self and atleast a psychological pull towards it. Mayhaps when her true self is stimulated by Impact shenanigans, she can't resist the pull any longer?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Nov 21, 2012 2:20 pm Eliaskar wrote: How come nothing happened when she went to pull out the Lance of Longinus out of Lilith in episode 22 then?

[]:             Quote Post by Linh » Thu Nov 22, 2012 5:34 am I understand that what the Angels need Adam, and Kaworu was tricked into thinking Lilith as Adam. What I mean is that Angel+Adam ( same type seed + same type seed ) might not be the directly cause for an Impact, and there is a possiblity that all the theory of Adam+Angel=I is just something being held for some certain purposes. As what I remember, it was never mentioned that the reunion of Angel and Adam would actually lead to the Impact, since we've never got to see one. But all three of the Impact that had happened were always the recipe "Seed type A + Seed type B=Impact (at least 1 original body is needed)"
